name: Memoization
description: Questions on caching function results and lru_cache.

questions.question: Memoization is used to:
choices: Cache function results to avoid recomputation
questions.explanation: Memoization stores results for given arguments and returns the cached value on repeated calls.

questions.question: functools.lru_cache(maxsize=128) by default:
questions.explanation: LRU stands for Least Recently Used; when full, the least recently used entry is evicted, so the cache keeps the most recently used.

questions.question: For lru_cache to work, arguments must be:
questions.explanation: Cache keys are derived from arguments; they must be hashable (e.g. no lists as args).

questions.question: Manual memoization for fib(n) typically uses:
choices: A dict mapping n to fib(n)
questions.explanation: A dict maps n to the computed fib(n) so repeated n returns the cached value.

questions.question: lru_cache(None) means:
questions.explanation: maxsize=None means the cache can grow without bound (no LRU eviction).
